message:
feat(search): fail open to lexical on explicit --mode hybrid when semantic assets are missing

Previously the "search command falls back to lexical instead of erroring
out" branch was gated on `default_hybrid_requested`, defined as
"mode was defaulted AND mode is Hybrid". That meant:

    cass search "foo"                 → defaulted Hybrid → fail open ✓
    cass search "foo" --mode hybrid   → explicit Hybrid  → hard error ✗

The hard error surfaced as a "Run 'cass models install'" message even
though the user's intent — "use semantic if you can, otherwise fall
back" — was the same in both cases. The explicit form punished the
user for being explicit, especially painful for hooks that always pass
`--mode hybrid` defensively.

Define the intent at the type level:

- Introduce `SearchModeMeta::fail_open_on_semantic_unavailable()` that
  returns true for any `SearchMode::Hybrid`, regardless of whether it
  was defaulted. Semantic (`--mode semantic`) is still treated as a
  hard requirement because the user is asking for semantic
  specifically.
- Rename the local in `run_cli_search` from `default_hybrid_requested`
  to `hybrid_fail_open` and thread it through all three fail-open
  sites: semantic context rejection during setup, semantic context
  unavailable at search time, and "unavailable"/"no embedder" at
  execution time.
- Update the hybrid-fail-open code comment to reflect the new intent
  ("preference for semantic refinement, not a strict dependency").
- Add a unit test `hybrid_search_intent_is_fail_open_even_when_explicit`
  in the `subcommand_robot_output_tests` module that covers the three
  cases: defaulted Hybrid, explicit Hybrid, explicit Semantic.
- Add an integration test
  `search_robot_meta_reports_explicit_hybrid_fail_open` in
  `tests/cli_robot.rs` that runs `cass search ... --mode hybrid
  --robot-meta --json` against the search_demo_data fixture (which has
  no semantic assets) and asserts the robot metadata reports:
    - requested_search_mode == "hybrid" (intent preserved)
    - search_mode         == "lexical" (realized tier)
    - mode_defaulted      == false     (explicit flag)
    - fallback_tier       == "lexical" (fail-open reason recorded)
    - semantic_refinement == false     (no semantic pass happened)

So: user gets what they want. Hooks that pass `--mode hybrid` defensively
no longer break when the environment lacks semantic assets, and the
robot metadata still surfaces the realized tier for observability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
